title: Size of symmetric intersecting families in $[k]^n$
statement_text: How large can a symmetric, intersecting subfamily of $[k]^n$ really be (for fixed $k \geq 3$)?
context_text: Theorem 1.1 establishes that any symmetric intersecting $A \subset [k]^n$ has size $o(k^n)$, but the precise asymptotics remain open. The best constructions known to the authors are set-intersecting families, e.g., those based on lines of a projective plane $PG(2,q)$, yielding size roughly $k^{n-\sqrt{n}}$.
notes: PDF source — math notation may be garbled. No labeled environment; described by the authors as 'the most obvious question raised by the present work' in Section 4.
paper_title: On symmetric intersecting families of vectors

title: Largest symmetric intersecting families are set-intersecting
statement_text: It seems possible (but maybe impossible to prove) that the largest symmetric intersecting families in $[k]^n$ are set-intersecting.
context_text: A family $A \subset [k]^n$ is set-intersecting if there exists a symmetric intersecting family $\mathcal{B}$ of subsets of $[n]$ and $\ell \in [k]$ such that $x \in A$ if and only if there is some $B \in \mathcal{B}$ with $x_i = \ell$ for all $i \in B$. The authors note that all their best constructions have this form, motivating the conjecture.
notes: PDF source — math notation may be garbled. Stated with the explicit hedge 'seems possible (but maybe impossible to prove)'; no labeled environment.
paper_title: On symmetric intersecting families of vectors

title: Polynomial improvement on symmetric intersecting family size bound
statement_text: Show that there exist constants $c, \delta > 0$ (possibly depending on $k$) such that for any symmetric intersecting $A \subset [k]^n$, we have $\log_k |A| \leq n - cn^{\delta}$.
context_text: This is posed in Section 4 as a concrete and more modest goal, falling short of identifying the exact extremal size: even a polynomial gain over the $o(k^n)$ bound of Theorem 1.1, expressed as $\log_k |A| \leq n - cn^\delta$, would be a significant advance.
notes: PDF source — math notation may be garbled. No labeled environment; introduced with 'it would be very interesting to at least show' in Section 4.
paper_title: On symmetric intersecting families of vectors
